history.content:
<thought>
I am at row 07 column D, facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The cells to my right side appear to be open floor, and turning right will let me move safely along the bottom corridor.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am at row 07 column D, still facing upward. The cell directly in front of me at row 06 column D is safe floor, while the cell to my right is a wall and the cells to my left are separated by lava in column C. Moving forward is the safe action that advances me through the open corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward with a safe floor cell directly ahead at row 06 column D. The right side is blocked by a wall, and moving forward continues through the only open corridor while avoiding the lava band visible above. Advancing one step is safe.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead at row 06 column D is safe floor. The right side remains blocked by a wall, and the lava line is still farther ahead, so moving forward keeps me in the safe corridor and progresses upward.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is safe floor at row 06 column D. However, one more step beyond that would face a lava barrier across row 05, so I should still advance now to reach the last safe cell before deciding a turn.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is row 06 column D, which is lava, so moving forward would be fatal. The cell to the right is a wall, leaving the safe option to turn left and prepare to move into the open floor corridor on the left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
After turning left, I am facing toward the open corridor. The cell directly ahead is safe floor, and moving forward advances me through the only clear path while avoiding the lava columns.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am still facing up the central safe corridor. The cell directly ahead is safe floor, and continuing forward moves me closer to the visible goal without entering lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am still aligned with the safe vertical corridor, and the cell directly ahead is open floor. Moving forward continues toward the goal while avoiding the lava on both sides.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is now visible two rows ahead and to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is safe floor and keeps me advancing up the only open corridor. Moving forward is safe and brings me closer before turning toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is one row ahead and two columns to the right. The cell directly in front of me is safe floor, and moving forward will place me on the same row as the goal without touching lava. That is the best next step.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am directly below a safe floor cell, and the goal is on the row ahead to the right. The cells to my left and right on my current row are lava, so the only safe progress is to move forward onto the floor corridor.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible two cells to my right on the same row. The cell directly ahead is a wall, so moving forward is impossible. Turning right will face the open corridor toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is straight ahead two cells away in the direction I am facing. The cell directly in front is a safe floor cell, while turning would move me away and risks the lava on the right side. Advancing is the best safe step toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The goal is directly ahead one cell away. The cell in front is the goal and is safe to enter, while the cells to the right are lava. I should move forward to reach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
